div.h1: File Descriptor Table Evolution: cmd1 | cmd2
div.h3: Tracing Process Isolation, Resource Inheritance, and FD Redirection

g.text: 3 | PIPE_R | Pipe Read End (Created via pipe())
4 | PIPE_W | Pipe Write End (Created via pipe())

div.p: Context: Shell is idle, waiting for command input.

ul.li: FDs 0, 1, 2 point to the controlling terminal (/dev/tty).
Returns two new FDs in the Shell's table.
FD 3: Read end of the buffer.
FD 4: Write end of the buffer.
Child 1 inherits a complete copy of Shell's FD table.
Both Shell and Child 1 now hold references to the pipe.
Risk: If references aren't closed, EOF will never be sent.
close(3) & close(4): Removes redundant pipe refs.
Result: cmd1's stdout now feeds directly into the pipe.
Child 2 also inherits FDs 3 and 4 from the Parent Shell.
CRITICAL: If Child 2 forgets to close(4), it holds an open write end.
The Hang: Even if cmd1 finishes, the pipe stays "open" because Child 2 could theoretically write to it. cmd2 hangs waiting for EOF.

div.p: Action: Shell closes its pipe FDs. Child 2 rebinds stdin.

ul.li: Success: Only Child 1 can write; Only Child 2 can read.

div.p: If the Parent Shell forgets to close(p[1]):

ol.li: Kernel checks "Who has Write Ends open?"
It sees the Parent Shell still has p[1].
It does not send EOF to cmd2.
